claims.claim: Decentralized infrastructure networks (DePIN) — compute, storage, wireless, energy
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: ETH as foundational settlement layer / productive monetary asset
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on: deploy through the winter in primary markets
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Strong embrace of a multi-chain / cross-chain interoperability thesis (LayerZero: 'no longer any question that the future of crypto and web3 is multi-chain')
Engagement with the modular vs. monolithic blockchain architecture debate and Solana (debate with Anatoly Yakovenko)
Explicit championing of Ethereum L2 scaling (Arbitrum, Optimism, EigenLayer) as the path to permissionless innovation
Creator-economy / web3 music & NFT focus (Sound.xyz Series A, Tiers launch)
Crypto–web2 payments integration as a theme (Loop Crypto + Stripe)
Interest in decentralized information markets (Freatic investment)
notes: The AI×crypto and ZK theses were the most heavily confirmed, with Gensyn (decentralized AI compute, a DePIN+AI play), the AI×crypto podcast, zkML discussion, Iron Fish, Aztec, and Lasso/Jolt all directly supporting them. ETH-as-settlement-layer was confirmed at the stance level via extensive L2/danksharding/EigenLayer advocacy even though the specific Shapella catalyst wasn't mentioned. Decentralized social/consumer confirmed via Sound.xyz and XMTP. Risk-on confirmed by active deal-leading. All three 'concerned' items (CEX custody, stablecoin banking risk, US regulatory overhang) and the DeFi excitement went entirely unaddressed in the window — none contradicted, just silent. The window's actual content skewed toward multi-chain infra, L2 scaling, and creator economy, which the prediction under-weighted.
